question: What does BULK COLLECT do?
options: Commits many rows at once
Fetches multiple rows from a query into a collection in a single operation, instead of row-by-row
Locks a whole table
Creates an index in bulk

explanation.uz: BULK COLLECT so'rov natijasidagi ko'p qatorni bir amalda kolleksiyaga o'qiydi — har qatorni alohida FETCH qilish o'rniga. Bu PL/SQL va SQL dvigatellari orasidagi kontekst almashinuvini keskin kamaytiradi.
explanation.en: BULK COLLECT fetches many rows of a query into a collection in one operation, instead of fetching row by row. This sharply cuts context switches between the PL/SQL and SQL engines.

question: What does FORALL do?
options: It is a loop that runs SELECT statements
It sends a whole collection of DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) to the SQL engine in one bulk operation
It declares a cursor
It commits a transaction

explanation.uz: FORALL kolleksiyadagi barcha elementlar uchun DML'ni (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) bitta bulk amalda SQL dvigateliga uzatadi. U — sikl emas, balki bitta bulk DML operatori.
explanation.en: FORALL sends DML (INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) for all elements of a collection to the SQL engine as a single bulk operation. It is not a loop — it is one bulk DML statement.

question: What is the key difference between a normal FOR loop doing DML and FORALL?
options: There is no difference
A FOR loop does one context switch per row (slow); FORALL passes all rows in one switch (much faster) — but FORALL only performs DML, not arbitrary logic
FORALL is slower than a FOR loop
A FOR loop cannot contain DML

explanation.uz: FOR sikl har qatorda PL/SQL↔SQL kontekst almashinuvi qiladi (sekin). FORALL barcha qatorni bitta almashinuvda uzatadi (ancha tez). Lekin FORALL faqat DML bajaradi — ichida ixtiyoriy mantiq yozib bo'lmaydi.
explanation.en: A FOR loop does one PL/SQL↔SQL context switch per row (slow). FORALL passes all rows in a single switch (much faster). But FORALL only performs DML — you can't put arbitrary logic inside it.

question: What is the main reason BULK COLLECT / FORALL are faster than row-by-row processing?
options: They avoid COMMITs
They cut the number of context switches between the PL/SQL and SQL engines — the main cost of row-by-row processing
They enforce constraints
They build indexes automatically

explanation.uz: Qator-ba-qator ishlashning asosiy narxi — PL/SQL va SQL dvigatellari orasidagi kontekst almashinuvlari. BULK COLLECT/FORALL ularni bir necha bittaga tushiradi, shuning uchun tez ishlaydi.
explanation.en: The main cost of row-by-row work is the context switches between the PL/SQL and SQL engines. BULK COLLECT/FORALL reduce those to a few, which is why they are fast.

question: A very large BULK COLLECT can exhaust PGA memory. How do you bound it?
options: You cannot — it always loads everything
Use the LIMIT clause in a loop: FETCH ... BULK COLLECT INTO ... LIMIT n
Add more indexes
Call COMMIT more often

explanation.uz: Katta natijani bir zumda o'qish PGA'ni to'ldiradi. Yechim — FETCH ... BULK COLLECT INTO ... LIMIT n bilan siklda porsiyalab (masalan 1000 tadan) o'qish.
explanation.en: Loading a huge result at once can exhaust the PGA. The fix is to fetch in batches inside a loop with FETCH ... BULK COLLECT INTO ... LIMIT n (e.g. 1000 at a time).

question: What is EXECUTE IMMEDIATE used for?
options: To make static SQL run faster
To run dynamic SQL or DDL built as a string at run time (e.g. 'CREATE TABLE ...', or SQL whose text isn't known until run time)
To COMMIT a transaction
To open a cursor

explanation.uz: EXECUTE IMMEDIATE dinamik SQL yoki DDL'ni satr sifatida ish vaqtida bajaradi — masalan PL/SQL ichida DDL ('CREATE TABLE ...') yoki matni oldindan noma'lum so'rov.
explanation.en: EXECUTE IMMEDIATE runs dynamic SQL or DDL built as a string at run time — e.g. DDL inside PL/SQL ('CREATE TABLE ...'), or a query whose text isn't known until run time.

question: What is a major downside of dynamic SQL that concatenates values into the string?
options: It is always faster than static SQL
It is exposed to SQL injection and forces a hard parse each time — use bind variables (the USING clause) to avoid both
It cannot run DDL
It commits automatically

explanation.uz: Qiymatlarni satrga ulab yozilgan dinamik SQL — SQL injection xavfi va har safar hard parse. Ikkalasini ham bind o'zgaruvchilar (USING bandi) hal qiladi: EXECUTE IMMEDIATE '... WHERE id = :1' USING v_id.
explanation.en: Dynamic SQL that concatenates values risks SQL injection and forces a hard parse each time. Bind variables (the USING clause) fix both: EXECUTE IMMEDIATE '... WHERE id = :1' USING v_id.

options: It commits the rows that were inserted before the error
It undoes changes back to the savepoint (the failed insert's effects), keeping earlier work and leaving the transaction open
It rolls back every transaction in the database
It does nothing — SAVEPOINT can't be rolled back to

explanation.uz: ROLLBACK TO sp_before_insert faqat savepoint'dan keyingi o'zgarishlarni (muvaffaqiyatsiz insert ta'sirini) bekor qiladi; undan oldingi ishlar saqlanadi va tranzaksiya ochiq qoladi. Keyin yakuniy COMMIT yoki ROLLBACK kerak.
explanation.en: ROLLBACK TO sp_before_insert undoes only changes after the savepoint (the failed insert's effects); earlier work is kept and the transaction stays open. A final COMMIT or ROLLBACK is still needed.
